rationale: Full re-review after PR #40 matcher change (_KEY_CHARS_NO_DOT became _KEY_CHARS_NO_DOT_NO_COLON; key pattern gained the (?!:[before]) lookahead) and the two new test scenarios. All probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/gaps.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) missing required reference -> non-zero: failing branch 'return 3' -> 'return 0' KILLED (partial, bare-prose, prefix, and colon-qualified docs all assert exit 3); (2) fully covered -> zero: success branch 'return 0' -> 'return 3' KILLED (full.md and annotated.md assert 0); (3) bare prose mention is not a reference: matching whole content instead of [[..]] blocks (referenced = content) KILLED by the 'We do not address X-2' doc; (4) longer sibling never satisfies shorter ([[X-12]] vs X-1): dropping the after-boundary lookahead (?![after]) KILLED by the only-[[X-12]] doc; (5) NEW - [[KEY: annotation]] counts as a reference to KEY: restoring colon to the after-boundary class ('A-Za-z0-9_\-' -> 'A-Za-z0-9_:\-') KILLED by the annotated [[FDA-SW:sdmp: pointer]] doc asserting 0; (6) NEW - colon-QUALIFIED longer key never satisfies its prefix: dropping the second lookahead (?!:[before]) KILLED by the [[FDA-SW:sdmp]]-only doc asserting 3 against a checklist requiring FDA-SW. The human-readable listing of missing keys remains exercised under DI-12; here the audit's contract is its exit status, pinned in both directions and at both boundary allowances.
